p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot; dense foliage cluster; leaves remain turgid with consistent green pigmentation.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Stable across the 5-day sequence. No significant morphological shifts observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Resilient. The plant maintains structural integrity despite the fluctuating VPD environment.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot; two primary leaves visible; central growth point remains active.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: The leaf margins show a slight stabilization in color compared to the T-4 baseline, where dehydration was more pronounced.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Recovering. The reduction in leaf-margin crisping suggests the recent AC cooling trial is positively impacting the micro-climate.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot; two leaves present; white rabbit anchor (5cm) remains in position.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The leaf with the central necrosis has not progressed in lesion size since T-4. Posture remains consistent.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Stable. The necrosis is contained, indicating the plant has reached a state of equilibrium with current humidity levels.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Black pot; small seedling near the rim; single elongated leaf structure.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: The seedling has maintained its position and orientation relative to the pot rim throughout the observation window.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Stable. No signs of chlorosis or wilting; growth is slow but steady.

biome_observations.soil_surface: Presence of white particulate matter (perlite/top-dressing) is consistent across all pots. No fungal blooms or surface crusting detected.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean; no debris or water spills noted.

temporal_deltas.sequence_summary: The transition from T-4 to CURRENT shows a clear reduction in heat-stress indicators following the AC cooling trial initiation.
temporal_deltas.notable_change: The stabilization of p2's leaf margins is the most significant physiological improvement observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a 'Stabilization Phase'. The cooling trial (25C) appears to have mitigated the VPD-driven stress observed in the earlier images.

anomalies.sensor_status: A5 sensor remains a point of divergence; visual data contradicts the sensor's moisture readings, confirming the previous recommendation to rely on visual assessment.
anomalies.user_action_confirmation: The white particulate matter on the soil surface is confirmed as a successful application of top-dressing/soil amendment, not a physiological anomaly.

vision_report.narrative_description: The garden is responding well to the environmental adjustments. The cooling trial has successfully halted the progression of leaf-margin dehydration in p2. p1 and p3 remain stable, showing no signs of further decline. The biome is currently balanced, with no new stressors identified.
